name: DREsS CASE Corruption-Based EFL Essay Augmentation
population_or_context: English-as-a-foreign-language undergraduate essay writing scored on content, organization, and language rubrics.
sample_size: DREsS includes 48.9K samples total, including 2,279 real classroom essays, 6,515 standardized samples, and 40,185 CASE synthetic samples.
methods: CASE begins with well-written essays and corrupts content, organization, or language at sentence level to generate synthetic essays across score ranges.
education_context: Rubric-based automated essay scoring for EFL writing education.
response_origin: Hybrid: real classroom essays, standardized existing essay data, and CASE-generated synthetic essays.
rubric_label_space: Three rubric dimensions: content, organization, and language; scores from 0 to 5.
label_source: Real classroom essays were scored by English education experts; CASE synthetic scores are constructed through controlled corruption from high-scoring essays.
generation_model: CASE corruption algorithm; gpt-4o used as a comparator in quality checks, not as the main DREsS_CASE generator.
generation_method: Rule-based corruption of high-scoring essays through sentence substitution, sentence swaps, and ungrammatical sentence substitution.
prompt_strategy: Not applicable to CASE; gpt-4o comparison used persona and example-prompted generation.
evaluation_method: Rubric-based AES baselines were trained with DREsS variants and evaluated with QWK on real classroom validation and test sets; synthetic quality was compared with gpt-4o generation.
scorer_use: Training augmentation and score-distribution balancing for rubric-based AES.
real_response_comparator: Yes. Synthetic or standardized training data were evaluated through model performance on DREsS_New real classroom essays.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: The paper reports scarce low-score samples and a need for usable rubric-based AES datasets.
synthetic_data_research_role: CASE generated balanced synthetic training samples across score ranges to improve rubric-based scorer training.
reported_metrics: DREsS_CASE contains 40.1K synthetic samples; adding standardized and CASE data improved baseline results by 45.44%; CASE synthetic-label QWK reached 0.661 versus 0.225 average for gpt-4o generation.
limitations: CASE starts from well-written essays and may not represent all EFL learner patterns; the authors identify further optimization of corruption thresholds as future work.
